query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "does vla even know the basics? measuring commonsense and world knowled" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on a specific architecture (VLV auto-encoder) for cost-efficient captioning and knowledge distillation from diffusion models, which does not measure the "commonsense and world knowledge" capabilities of VLA models nor address the specific evaluation gap identified in the user's referenced work. It fails to satisfy criteria (a) or (b) as it does not study the same mechanism (knowledge measurement) or variables (commonsense/world knowledge benchmarks) and is not a foundational me
